documents.title: GRC Professional Workday Analysis
documents.executiveSummary: GRC professionals spend 41% of productive hours reconciling evidence across disconnected SaaS controls, audit exports, and board prep decks. Continuous integrity audits fail when telemetry, CRM pipeline truth, and regulatory mapping drift across tenant boundaries.
keyFindings: Morning stand-ups consume 47 minutes average when audit status is sourced from four or more SaaS consoles.
Evidence re-keying into board packets adds 120000000 cents of annualized labor-equivalent exposure for mid-market GRC teams.
Tenant-scoped RLS and BIGINT-cent ledgers reduce reconciliation variance by eliminating float drift in ALE rollups.
Ironintel OSINT correlation and Ironscribe immutable exports compress board-report prep from days to deterministic checkpoints.

industryProfiles.narrativeSummary: Finance GRC teams face simultaneous SOC2, FFIEC, and insurance attestation cycles; fragmented SaaS GRC stacks inflate audit lag.
HIPAA and HITRUST evidence chains break when clinical SaaS vendors rotate APIs without DMZ sanitization.
High SaaS adoption velocity outpaces manual control mapping; continuous integrity audits must bind to agentic workforce checkpoints.
CMMC L3 programs require air-gapped evidence vaults; board reporting must cite immutable Ironscribe export hashes.
FedRAMP and state/local procurement cycles amplify vendor-change audit scope documented in Strategic Intel profiles.

documents.title: SaaS Disruption Memorandum
documents.executiveSummary: Seat-based GRC SaaS incumbents face category disruption from flat-fee, agent-orchestrated control planes that enforce Ironguard tenant isolation and whole-cent financial registers. Buyers prioritize continuous integrity over periodic audit snapshots.
keyFindings: Per-seat pricing models misalign with agentic workforce scaling; flat annual platform fees in integer cents remove float billing risk.
Consolidating five SaaS GRC tools into a sovereign control plane reduces duplicate evidence stores by 62%.
Board directors demand LP-10 config churn and LP-16 meta-audit visibility in a single executive packet.
SaaS vendors without DMZ ingress (Agent 14) fail enterprise procurement on data residency and sanitization grounds.

industryProfiles.narrativeSummary: Incumbent SaaS renewals face scrutiny when Ironframe demonstrates deterministic board-report ALE without spreadsheet exports.
Fastest SaaS disruption velocity; buyers pilot agentic GRC to replace manual Jira-to-audit bridges.

ragChunks.text: GRC professionals lose 41% of the workday to cross-SaaS reconciliation. Ironintel should prioritize OSINT that surfaces duplicate control owners across vendor tools before board-report generation.
LP-10 config churn and LP-16 meta-audit rows must cite Ironscribe immutable export hashes. Board packets reject float ALE; use BIGINT cent registers only.

Flat annual platform fees denominated in integer cents displace seat-based GRC SaaS. Strategic Intel Industry Profile should reflect saasDisruptionExposureIndex when scoring Technology and Finance sectors.
Enterprise buyers require Irongate DMZ sanitization on all external research ingress. Strategic Intel updates must pass Agent 14 schema validation before CRM persistence.
